title: Software Engineer I
mainText:
Principal Financial Group- Corporate Headquarters
Financial Services
Software Engineer I
Posted 1 week ago∙Apply by April 11, 2026 at 9:59 PM
Save
Share
Apply externally
At a glance
$58–79K/yr
Hybrid, based in Des Moines, IA
Work in person for part of the week, from the location
Job
Full-time
US work authorization required
Job description

What You'll Do

We are seeking a Software Engineer to join our Individual Solutions team within Retirement and Income Solutions. Individual Solutions IT focuses on using the best technology to solve complex problems while also maintaining high standards and limiting technical debt. The team’s focus is to help customers with their financial wellness by offering tools and products to help them achieve their goals.

With guidance and mentorship, the engineer will contribute to software development efforts while building foundational skills in source control, development tools, build systems, and automated testing best practices. This role also provides opportunities to participate in code and design reviews and to learn core technical design concepts in a collaborative team environment. The team works with a wide range of technologies, including AWS Cloud, Java, and Web development.

What You'll Do

Focus on growing as an engineer by learning existing tools, resources and processes. 
Develop the ability to write readable, maintainable, supportable code. Begin to learn security policies and standards. Take well-defined sub-tasks and completing these tasks.
Be introduced to the layers of a software development solution:
Run-time environment
Database
APIs
User interface and user experience
Quality assurance, automated testing
Security concerns throughout the program
Consider established controls, policies, regulations and recommend mitigation strategies during development. Understand customer and business needs. Develop knowledge of a single layer of the solution.
Participate in code/design reviews and learn technical design. Begin learning business and technical strategies.
Stay current and engaged on industry trends.
Assist in communicating technical concepts to business stakeholders as well as communicating business objectives to the technical team.
Perform other job-related duties or special projects as required.

Operating at the intersection of financial services and technology, Principal builds financial tools that help our customers live better lives. We take pride in being a purpose-led firm, motivated by our mission to make financial security accessible to all. Our mission, integrity, and customer focus have made us a trusted leader for more than 140 years!

Who You Are

Associate or bachelor's degree preferably in a computer science, technology, engineering or math-related field or equivalent experience
Proficiency with database applications, knowledge of mainframe, distributed and/or web programming language
Basic planning, organizational, problem-solving, analytical, decision-making and communication skills required
Must be able to maintain a high degree of accuracy and confidentiality
Must have the desire and ability to learn new technology and continuously grow
Ability to work on cross-functional teams and collaborate with both IT and non-IT partners
Must be able to provide on-call support on a rotational basis

Skills That Will Help You Stand Out

Basic knowledge of insurance and financial services products
Ability to obtain certification(s) in an IT related field
Leadership experience
Excellent time management skills

Salary Range Information

Salary ranges below reflect targeted base salaries. Non-sales positions have the opportunity to participate in a bonus program. Sales positions are eligible for sales incentives, and in some instances a bonus plan, whereby total compensation may far exceed base salary depending on individual performance. Actual compensation for all roles will be based upon geographic location, work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer.

Salary Range (Non-Exempt expressed as hourly; Exempt expressed as yearly)

$58900 - $79800 / year

Time Off Program

Flexible Time Off (FTO) is provided to salaried (exempt) employees and provides the opportunity to take time away from the office with pay for vacation, personal or short-term illness. Employees don’t accrue a bank of time off under FTO and there is no set number of days provided.

Pension Eligible

Yes

Location & Work Environment

This role will be located at our corporate headquarters in Des Moines, Iowa and offers a hybrid work arrangement (blending at least three office days in a typical workweek).

Work Authorization/Sponsorship

At this time, we're not considering applicants that need any type of immigration sponsorship (additional work authorization or permanent work authorization) now or in the future to work in the United States. This includes, but IS NOT LIMITED TO: F1-OPT, F1-CPT, H-1B, TN, L-1, J-1, etc. For additional information around work authorization needs please use the following links.

Nonimmigrant Workers and Green Card for Employment-Based Immigrants

Investment Code of Ethics

For Principal Asset Management positions, you’ll need to follow an Investment Code of Ethics related to personal and business conduct as well as personal trading activities for you and members of your household. These same requirements may also apply to other positions across the organization.

Experience Principal

At Principal, we value connecting on both a personal and professional level. Together, we’re imagining a more purpose-led future for financial services – and that starts with you. Our success depends on the unique experiences, backgrounds, and talents of our interns.

Principal is an Equal Opportunity Employer

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status.

Posting Window

We will accept applications for 3 full days following the Original Posting Date, after which the posting may remain open or be removed based upon applications received. If we choose to post the job again, we will accept additional applications for at least 1 full day following the Most Recently Posted Date. Please submit applications in a timely manner as there is no guarantee the posting will be available beyond the applicable deadline.

Original Posting Date

3/10/2026

Most Recently Posted Date

3/10/2026

About the employer
Principal Financial Group- Corporate Headquarters

Financial Services

Follow
10,000-25,000 employees

Des Moines, IA

Principal Financial Group® is dedicated to improving the wealth and well-being of people and businesses around the world—helping more than 62M customers plan, protect, invest, and retire as of December 31, 2022. Along the way, we commit to supporting the communities where we do business. Improving our planet. And building a diverse, inclusive workforce.

We’re proud to be recognized as a Best Place to Work in Money Management by Pensions & Investments for the 11th consecutive year, an Ethisphere World’s Most Ethical Companies for the 12th time and as Forbes The Best Employers for Diversity 2023.

Disclosure:
Principal® is an Affirmative Action and Equal Opportunity Employer and an E-Verify participant. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to of age, race, color, religion, gender identity, gender expression, pregnancy, national origin, citizenship status, disability, genetic characteristics, sexual orientation, marital status, domestic partner status, military status, protected veteran status, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Principal will never ask for money during any stage of the employment application or hiring process. If you're asked to send money or share personal financial information as part of next steps in the process, do not engage or respond. Contact Principal Human Resources at 833-261-3736 and your local law enforcement.
